title: Sulcorebutia arenacea
taxonomy: Sulcorebutia arenacea is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae. Within the genus Sulcorebutia, it is a type of small, globular cactus native to Bolivia. As a member of the Cactaceae family, it shares characteristics with other cacti, such as its ability to store water in its fleshy stems. The classification of Sulcorebutia arenacea is still subject to taxonomic revisions.

intro: Sulcorebutia arenacea, also known as the "sand dune living stone," is a species of cactus native to the deserts of Bolivia, characterized by its small, globular stems and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Sulcorebutia arenacea include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Sulcorebutia arenacea is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are employed to treat a range of health conditions, including eczema, acne, and minor infections.
